name: Michael Bunting
summary: Michael Bunting plays with a noticeable edge, ranking near the top of the league in both penalties taken and drawn. His high activity level reflects an aggressive, high-energy style that draws opponents into mistakes as often as it costs him.

name: Ilya Lyubushkin
summary: Ilya Lyubushkin logs significant minutes on the bench and can be turnover-prone, ranking high in giveaways. His defensive play leans physical but occasionally risky with the puck.

name: Brett Howden
summary: Brett Howden’s team performs well when he’s off the ice, reflected by an elite off-ice expected goals rate. He benefits from playing within a strong supporting group structure.

name: Adam Henrique
summary: Adam Henrique shows veteran responsibility, ranking near the top of the league in blocked shots. His off-ice possession and expected-goals metrics underline how stable the team remains around him.

name: Steven Lorentz
summary: Steven Lorentz brings physical presence, posting nearly 200 hits while managing significant bench time. His energy and willingness to engage are clear from his heavy contact totals.

name: Zack MacEwen
summary: Zack MacEwen’s off-ice possession metrics are strong, indicating his team controls play effectively in his absence. He contributes depth and grit when called upon.

name: Ryan Poehling
summary: Ryan Poehling provides reliable defensive effort, ranking among the top players in blocked shots. He shows strong two-way awareness and commitment to team defense.

name: Ian Cole
summary: Ian Cole is a shot-blocking machine, leading the league with over 200 blocks. His high penalty and giveaway counts reflect an intense, risk-heavy defensive style.

name: Barclay Goodrow
summary: Barclay Goodrow plays with grit and edge, ranking near the league leaders in penalties. His physical game fuels intensity but can occasionally draw whistles.

name: Adam Pelech
summary: Adam Pelech blends steady defense with sneaky playmaking, producing strong takeaway numbers and solid secondary assist totals from the blue line.

name: Jake McCabe
summary: Jake McCabe draws penalties at a high rate, showing his ability to pressure opponents and create power-play opportunities through smart, physical play.

name: Mathieu Olivier
summary: Mathieu Olivier is pure energy, leading the league with over 300 hits and 40 penalties. He blocks shots, draws calls, and plays with relentless physicality every shift.

name: Erik Gudbranson
summary: Erik Gudbranson’s off-ice metrics suggest the team maintains solid control when he’s resting. A rugged defender known more for toughness than puck control.

name: Mason Marchment
summary: Mason Marchment brings edge and emotion, sitting high in the league for penalty minutes. His aggressive forechecking defines his game.

name: Sean Durzi
summary: Sean Durzi contributes effectively to shot-quality differential, with a strong on-ice expected goals rate showing his ability to tilt play in his team’s favor.

name: Zach Werenski
summary: Zach Werenski had a monster season, leading all defenders in shot attempts, points, and goals. His volume shooting and consistent offensive drive make him one of the NHL’s elite blue-line threats.

name: Scott Laughton
summary: Scott Laughton delivers steady physical play, ranking high in hits. He adds grit and energy to his team’s forward group.

name: Christian Dvorak
summary: Christian Dvorak contributes strong defensive support, ranking near the top in blocked shots among forwards. His commitment in his own zone stands out.

name: Nick Foligno
summary: Nick Foligno combines leadership and physical play, with high on-ice goal totals and heavy hitting. His presence drives both energy and production.

name: Owen Power
summary: Owen Power continues to show offensive instincts, producing high rebound goal numbers and solid assist totals. His ability to generate mid-range chances adds real depth from the back end.

name: Dylan Holloway
summary: Dylan Holloway displays sharp defensive awareness, ranking high in takeaways. His ability to force turnovers and transition quickly stands out.

name: Roman Josi
summary: Roman Josi continues to dominate offensively from the blue line, leading defenders in high-danger shots and expected goals. His shot generation remains among the league’s best.

name: Luke Hughes
summary: Luke Hughes made a strong two-way impact, racking up assists and blocked shots. His offensive touch paired with responsible defensive play gives him a well-rounded profile early in his career.

name: Connor Brown
summary: Connor Brown’s off-ice metrics shine, with elite expected-goals and possession numbers indicating his team thrives during rest shifts. A reliable veteran in structure-heavy systems.

name: Michael Kesselring
summary: Michael Kesselring logs heavy minutes and brings a physical edge. His penalty rate shows a willingness to play tough, even if it toes the line.

name: Frank Vatrano
summary: Frank Vatrano’s blend of aggression and offense stands out. He draws plenty of whistles while generating rebounds and second-chance looks at an elite rate.

name: Brock Faber
summary: Brock Faber’s on-ice expected goals numbers highlight his impact in driving play and suppressing opponent quality. He anchors defensive possessions effectively.

name: Kirill Kaprizov
summary: Kirill Kaprizov continues to dominate offensive play-driving metrics. His on-ice expected goals percentage ranks near the league’s top, reflecting elite scoring influence.

name: Anze Kopitar
summary: Anze Kopitar remains a consistent finisher, posting one of the league’s top rebound goal totals. His experience and net-front timing keep him dangerous every night.

name: Shayne Gostisbehere
summary: Shayne Gostisbehere drives play at an elite level, topping the charts in Corsi, Fenwick, and expected-goals percentages. His puck movement keeps his team in control throughout games.

name: Jackson LaCombe
summary: Jackson LaCombe’s shooting stats jump off the page — elite in rebound goals and mid-range shot generation. His offensive instincts are developing fast for a young defender.

name: Mikael Granlund
summary: Mikael Granlund’s playmaking shows up clearly in expected-goals metrics, where his on-ice numbers rank among the best in the league. A creative force at both five-on-five and power play.

name: Brendan Gallagher
summary: Brendan Gallagher continues to play high-intensity minutes, ranking high in total bench time. His endurance and work rate remain key to his gritty game.

name: Samuel Girard
summary: Samuel Girard’s off-ice Fenwick rates show his team maintains good control when he rests, suggesting balanced deployment and reliable performance shifts.

name: Owen Tippett
summary: Owen Tippett’s willingness to block shots and generate low-danger volume shows his mix of defensive effort and offensive pressure. A high-effort, two-way winger.

name: Simon Edvinsson
summary: Simon Edvinsson is beginning to create more dangerous looks offensively, ranking well in high-danger shot attempts for a young blueliner.

name: Leon Draisaitl
summary: Leon Draisaitl is a powerhouse scorer, finishing among league leaders in goals and points. His ability to dominate possession and create high-quality chances remains unmatched.

name: Alex Pietrangelo
summary: Alex Pietrangelo’s off-ice expected goals metrics show how his structured defensive play supports a stable team environment when he’s rotated out.

name: Victor Olofsson
summary: Victor Olofsson’s on-ice expected goals rate reflects strong offensive efficiency. He contributes effectively to team scoring when in the lineup.

name: Brayden Schenn
summary: Brayden Schenn plays a hard-nosed game, ranking high in hits. His combination of leadership and physicality makes him a tone-setter for the Blues.

name: Kris Letang
summary: Kris Letang remains an offensive driver from the back end, generating second-chance looks and maintaining top rebound creation among defensemen.

name: Luke Evangelista
summary: Luke Evangelista’s strong Fenwick rate shows he helps his team control shot quality and possession when on the ice.

name: Anthony Cirelli
summary: Anthony Cirelli’s defensive commitment stands out, ranking near the top in blocked shots among forwards. He continues to anchor the Lightning’s two-way play.

name: Tim Sttzle
summary: Tim Stützle’s elite penalty-drawing numbers and strong possession metrics highlight his dynamic puck control and ability to create offensive zone pressure.

name: Evgenii Dadonov
summary: Evgenii Dadonov maintains consistent usage, logging heavy bench time as part of a deep forward rotation.

name: Martin Fehrvry
summary: Martin Fehérváry’s off-ice expected goals rate reflects strong team stability around his shifts, showing dependable defensive structure.

name: Alex Newhook
summary: Alex Newhook maintains a limited role, finding himself on the bench for most of each game.

name: Luke Schenn
summary: Luke Schenn remains one of hockey’s most physical defenders, leading the league with 300 hits. A steady presence known for toughness and durability.

name: K'Andre Miller
summary: K’Andre Miller continues to impress with elite takeaway numbers. His active stick and reach make him a standout defensive weapon.

name: Kaedan Korczak
summary: Kaedan Korczak’s high on-ice expected goals rate shows strong positional awareness and positive impact when deployed.

name: Jake Walman
summary: Jake Walman is an active offensive defenseman who is never shy about shooting the puck. He generates a high volume of shot attempts and shots on goal from the blue line, contributing to the offense while also chipping in as a playmaker.

name: Aleksander Barkov
summary: Aleksander Barkov is one of the premier two-way centers in the entire NHL. He is an elite possession driver who tilts the ice in his team's favor, a gifted playmaker, and a defensive stalwart who consistently ranks among the best forwards at creating takeaways.

name: Alex Vlasic
summary: Alex Vlasic is a promising young defenseman tasked with incredibly difficult minutes on a rebuilding team. Despite being on the ice for a league-high number of goals against, he excels individually with elite takeaway numbers and a strong commitment to blocking shots.

name: Jake Sanderson
summary: Jake Sanderson is a rising, high-event defenseman who is involved in all aspects of the game. He is an elite puck thief, a high-volume shooter, and a key playmaker, though he is also on the ice for a high number of quality chances against while logging tough matchups.

name: Zemgus Girgensons
summary: Zemgus Girgensons is a prototypical fourth-line energy player. He provides a physical presence with a high rate of hits while playing limited minutes in a depth role.

name: Kevin Bahl
summary: Kevin Bahl is a depth defenseman who provides size and a steady presence on the third pair. The team's strong possession metrics when he is on the bench suggest he is used effectively in a sheltered role.

name: David Pastrnak
summary: David Pastrnak is one of the most dynamic and dominant offensive players in the world. He is the ultimate high-volume shooter, an elite goal scorer and playmaker, and the engine of his team's offense every time he touches the puck.

name: Ryan Pulock
summary: Ryan Pulock is a steady, defense-first blueliner whose game is built around his commitment to team defense. His standout trait is his willingness to get in shooting lanes and block shots.

name: Spencer Stastney
summary: Spencer Stastney is a depth defenseman who fills out the defensive rotation. His team's excellent performance metrics when he is off the ice highlight the strength of Nashville's top pairings.

name: Adam Fantilli
summary: Adam Fantilli is a highly talented young center with immense offensive potential. He is already an effective goal scorer and a high-volume shooter, though his game still shows signs of youth with a high number of turnovers and blocked shots.

name: Parker Kelly
summary: Parker Kelly is a diligent fourth-line forward who excels in a defensive role. He provides value with an elite willingness to block shots while playing limited, specialized minutes for a deep team.

name: Mikey Anderson
summary: Mikey Anderson is a reliable, steady defenseman on a team that excels at puck possession. The Kings' ability to maintain elite control of the game when he is on the bench speaks to his role as a key contributor to a very deep and effective blue line.

name: Juraj Slafkovsk
summary: Juraj Slafkovský is a developing power forward who uses his large frame to his advantage. He is a high-volume hitter who plays a physical brand of hockey and contributes on both sides of the puck.

name: Nikolaj Ehlers
summary: Nikolaj Ehlers is a dynamic and creative offensive winger who consistently drives play in the right direction. He is an excellent playmaker whose line generates a high quantity and quality of scoring chances whenever he is on the ice.

name: Evan Rodrigues
summary: Evan Rodrigues is a versatile forward who makes a strong positive impact on puck possession. His team consistently controls the majority of shot attempts when he is on the ice, making him a valuable part of a deep forward corps.

name: Jake Guentzel
summary: Jake Guentzel is a truly elite goal scorer and one of the most effective net-front players in the NHL. He thrives in the toughest areas of the ice, generating a massive number of high-danger shots and excelling at converting rebounds into goals.

name: Tyler Kleven
summary: Tyler Kleven is a young defenseman who is still finding his role at the NHL level. He has served primarily as a depth option, spending significant time as a healthy scratch.

name: Sean Monahan
summary: Sean Monahan has carved out a niche as one of the absolute best in the league at scoring 'garbage' goals. His elite, singular skill is his ability to find and bury loose pucks and rebounds around the crease.

name: Jack Eichel
summary: Jack Eichel is an elite, play-driving number one center who produces offense at a prolific rate. He is a masterful playmaker who consistently puts up points and elevates the play of his linemates, leading to dominant on-ice results for his team.

name: Shane Pinto
summary: Shane Pinto is a forward who knows how to get to the right spots on the ice. He generates a high percentage of his offense from prime scoring areas right in front of the net.

name: Evgeni Malkin
summary: Evgeni Malkin continues to be a high-event player late in his storied career. He remains skilled at drawing penalties and stripping pucks, but his creativity and risk-taking also result in a high number of turnovers.

name: Jason Robertson
summary: Jason Robertson is an elite offensive winger and a prolific point producer. He is particularly adept at scoring on rebounds around the net and consistently puts up high-end goal and assist totals, even if it comes with the risk of turning the puck over.

name: Steven Stamkos
summary: Steven Stamkos remains a dangerous offensive threat, generating a high number of quality shots from the slot. He plays a high-event game, often deployed in tough matchups that lead to goals against while also taking a significant number of penalties.

name: Brandt Clarke
summary: Brandt Clarke is a highly effective puck-moving defenseman who tilts the ice in his team's favor. When he is on the ice, his team controls the flow of play, dominating both the quantity and quality of shots. He also plays with an edge, taking his share of penalties.

name: Chandler Stephenson
summary: Chandler Stephenson is a reliable two-way center tasked with difficult defensive assignments. He is an effective playmaker and puck thief, but is often on the ice against top opposition, resulting in a high rate of scoring chances against.

name: Nikita Kucherov
summary: Nikita Kucherov is a generational offensive talent and arguably the best playmaker in the world. He produces points at a historic rate and is the engine behind a league-best offense, handling the puck so often that his high giveaway numbers are simply a byproduct of his dominance.

name: Ross Colton
summary: Ross Colton is a valuable depth forward who adds to an already potent lineup. His team's ability to maintain strong possession even when he is on the bench highlights Colorado's incredible overall strength.

name: Seth Jones
summary: Seth Jones is a minute-munching defenseman who can contribute offensively by activating from the point and setting up teammates. He is trusted with heavy, defensively challenging minutes, which often results in being on the ice for a high number of goals against.

name: Dmitry Kulikov
summary: Dmitry Kulikov is a veteran defenseman who provides reliable depth on a powerhouse team. His excellent off-ice metrics show he is a steady presence who can fill in seamlessly while the team's top players drive the results.

name: Jesperi Kotkaniemi
summary: Jesperi Kotkaniemi is a center who fits perfectly into his team's puck-dominant system. Whether he is on the ice or off, his team controls the flow of shot attempts at an elite level, making him an effective component of a relentless four-line attack.

name: Mike Matheson
summary: Mike Matheson is a workhorse number one defenseman who logs the toughest minutes imaginable. He is constantly deployed against top opposition, leading to a league-high rate of chances and goals against, but he battles hard, excelling at takeaways and blocking shots.

name: Joel Edmundson
summary: Joel Edmundson is a physical, stay-at-home defenseman who provides veteran depth. He is used in a specific defensive role, and his team's strong possession numbers when he's on the bench reflect the strength of their top pairings.

name: Tony DeAngelo
summary: Tony DeAngelo is an offensive-minded defenseman whose value comes from his ability to help generate offense. When he is on the ice, his team consistently gets more unblocked shots toward the net than they allow.

name: Ben Chiarot
summary: Ben Chiarot is a rugged, physical defenseman who plays a tough, high-event game. He is a high-volume hitter who consistently takes and draws penalties, and is often deployed in difficult matchups that result in a high rate of chances against.

name: Clayton Keller
summary: Clayton Keller is the undisputed offensive engine for his team. He is an elite playmaker and point producer who drives incredibly strong on-ice results, consistently helping his team dominate possession and scoring chances while also drawing a high number of penalties.

name: Mark Scheifele
summary: Mark Scheifele is an elite offensive center with an exceptional nose for the net. He is the best in the league at converting rebounds, generates a huge volume of shots from the slot, and produces goals and points at a prolific rate.

name: Jordan Spence
summary: Jordan Spence is a skilled young defenseman who has a massive positive impact on puck possession. When he is on the ice, his team controls the game at an elite level, dominating both the quality and quantity of scoring chances.

name: Seth Jarvis
summary: Seth Jarvis is a tenacious forward who perfectly embodies his team's system. He is elite at getting shots off from the slot, drives possession at a remarkable rate, and is a key contributor to Carolina's relentless offensive pressure.

name: Mario Ferraro
summary: Mario Ferraro is a tough, durable defenseman who logs some of the most difficult minutes in the league. He is constantly on the ice against top lines, resulting in a high rate of goals against, but he battles hard with physicality and effective stick work.

name: Evan Bouchard
summary: Evan Bouchard is a transcendent offensive defenseman and a dominant force from the blue line. He controls the game at a historic level, with his team generating an overwhelming majority of the shots and scoring chances when he is on the ice. He is an elite playmaker and the engine of a historically great power play.

name: Emil Heineman
summary: Emil Heineman is a fourth-line winger whose primary role is to provide energy and physicality. His standout contribution is his high rate of hitting.

name: Auston Matthews
summary: Auston Matthews is a generational goal scorer who has also evolved into one of the league's best defensive forwards. He generates a massive volume of shots from the most dangerous areas of the ice and is also elite among forwards at blocking shots, making him a dominant force at both ends of the rink.

name: Jonas Siegenthaler
summary: Jonas Siegenthaler is a pure stay-at-home defenseman. He is tasked with a primarily defensive role, and the team's strong possession numbers when he's on the bench reflect the offensive talent of the players who get more favorable matchups.

name: Chris Tanev
summary: Chris Tanev is a premier shutdown defenseman whose entire game is built around courage and defensive positioning. He is one of the league's most prolific and fearless shot blockers, sacrificing his body to protect his net.

name: J.T. Compher
summary: J.T. Compher is a versatile and defensively reliable forward. A key part of his value comes from his willingness to get into shooting lanes, ranking among the top forwards in the league for blocked shots.

name: Pierre-Luc Dubois
summary: Pierre-Luc Dubois is a skilled power forward who plays a heavy, agitating game. He is an excellent playmaker who uses his size to his advantage, consistently drawing opponents into penalties while also taking his fair share.

name: Peyton Krebs
summary: Peyton Krebs is a tenacious depth forward who has mastered the art of getting under the opponent's skin. His greatest skill is his ability to draw penalties, ranking among the league's very best in that area.

name: Erik Haula
summary: Erik Haula is a dependable veteran forward who plays a middle-six role. His team's strong performance in expected goals when he is off the ice is a reflection of New Jersey's deep and talented forward group.

name: Marco Rossi
summary: Marco Rossi is a skilled young forward who has a positive effect on his team's offensive output. With him on the ice, his line consistently generates a higher quality of scoring chances than they allow.

name: Niko Mikkola
summary: Niko Mikkola is a big, bruising defenseman who plays a physically imposing style. He is a high-volume hitter who consistently takes and draws penalties, providing grit and toughness in a depth role for a very strong team.

name: Victor Hedman
summary: Victor Hedman remains one of the most dominant defensemen in hockey. He drives offense from the blue line with a heavy shot and sharp vision, producing high totals in both goals and assists. While his aggressive play can lead to turnovers, his ability to block shots and create chances more than makes up for it, anchoring Tampa Bay’s attack and defense alike.

name: Mikko Rantanen
summary: Mikko Rantanen is a complete offensive threat who can score from anywhere. His blend of power and finesse shows through his strong goal and assist numbers, as well as his high shot volume. He’s dangerous around the crease, creating rebounds and high-danger looks regularly. Though occasionally reckless with the puck, his ability to tilt the ice in his team’s favor is undeniable.

name: Radko Gudas
summary: Radko Gudas brings toughness and energy every night. He’s one of the most physical players in the league, throwing huge hits and blocking plenty of shots. While he takes his fair share of penalties, his presence keeps opponents honest and adds grit to Anaheim’s blue line.

name: Alexander Wennberg
summary: Alexander Wennberg plays a smart, defensive-minded game down the middle. He reads plays well, forcing turnovers and blocking shots to help out his goaltender. Though not flashy offensively, his reliability makes him a steady presence in San Jose’s lineup.

name: Rickard Rakell
summary: Rickard Rakell brings steady scoring and strong two-way play to Pittsburgh’s top six. He’s capable of finishing from mid-range and finding open teammates for scoring chances. While he occasionally misses the net, his work rate and ability to generate shots make him a consistent threat.

name: Marcus Foligno
summary: Marcus Foligno is the heart of Minnesota’s checking lines. He brings relentless physicality and never hesitates to finish a hit. His discipline and intensity make him a key presence in tight, hard-fought games.

name: Josh Doan
summary: Josh Doan has quickly emerged as a reliable young winger with excellent possession numbers. He helps his team control the puck and sustain offensive pressure, often outplaying opponents in transition and along the boards.

name: Olle Lycksell
summary: Olle Lycksell is still finding his place in Philadelphia’s lineup but shows promise in driving play the right direction. When he’s on the ice, the team generally controls more quality chances, a good sign for his long-term development.

name: Jonathan Drouin
summary: Jonathan Drouin has rediscovered his game with Colorado, excelling in puck control and offensive-zone pressure. His creativity helps sustain possession, and he’s played a strong supporting role alongside the team’s top talent.

name: Vasily Podkolzin
summary: Vasily Podkolzin plays a physical brand of hockey, bringing energy on every shift. He uses his frame effectively on the forecheck and along the boards, though he’s still looking to carve out a more consistent offensive role.

name: Joel Farabee
summary: Joel Farabee is still working to solidify his place in Calgary’s lineup. He’s often used in depth roles but brings good skating and awareness, with potential to become a reliable contributor in time.

name: Nick Suzuki
summary: Nick Suzuki continues to shine as Montreal’s top center. His vision and playmaking set him apart, and he’s capable of both creating and finishing plays. While he takes risks with the puck, his composure and consistency make him the heartbeat of the Canadiens’ offense.

name: Alex Ovechkin
summary: Alex Ovechkin remains one of the most dangerous goal scorers in the game. He fires pucks from everywhere, leading Washington’s attack with power and precision. Even as he ages, his knack for finding the net and generating offense is unmatched.

name: Sidney Crosby
summary: Sidney Crosby continues to defy time, producing at an elite level. His balance of skill, vision, and determination drives Pittsburgh’s offense. Whether setting up teammates or finishing plays himself, he remains one of the most complete centers in the NHL.

name: Michael McCarron
summary: Michael McCarron brings physicality and grit to Nashville’s lineup. He plays a heavy game, drawing and taking penalties in equal measure. While not known for scoring, he’s a tough presence who wears down opponents in defensive roles.

name: Tanner Jeannot
summary: Tanner Jeannot plays with an edge, delivering hits and battling in the dirty areas. He brings energy to every shift and provides secondary scoring when needed, embodying the hard-nosed identity of the Kings.

name: Brayden McNabb
summary: Brayden McNabb is a defensive anchor for Vegas. He blocks shots, plays physically, and makes smart, simple plays in his own zone. His defensive awareness allows his teammates to take more offensive risks with confidence.

name: Kyle MacLean
summary: Kyle MacLean hasn’t seen much ice time but continues to learn in the Islanders’ system. His role so far has been limited, yet he shows good structure and attention to detail when given opportunities.

name: Kevin Fiala
summary: Kevin Fiala is a dynamic offensive winger who thrives on speed and creativity. He’s always a shooting threat, putting up impressive shot totals and creating chances for himself and teammates. Though he can be turnover-prone, his offensive instincts make him a difference-maker.

name: Jack McBain
summary: Jack McBain plays a bruising, north-south style of hockey. He hits everything in sight and makes life tough for defenders. While his offense is still developing, his energy and size make him an important depth piece.

name: Alexander Romanov
summary: Alexander Romanov is a hard-hitting defender who plays with a mean streak. He blocks shots and punishes opponents physically, helping set the tone for New York’s defensive structure.

name: Colton Sissons
summary: Colton Sissons is a steady, defensive center who handles tough matchups. He’s reliable in his own end, blocks shots, and chips in with responsible, team-first hockey every night.

name: Tomas Nosek
summary: Tomas Nosek doesn’t light up the scoresheet, but when he’s off the ice, the team’s numbers drop. His defensive instincts, positioning, and work ethic help stabilize Florida’s depth lines.

name: Tyler Myers
summary: Tyler Myers plays an imposing, physical game on the back end. His size and reach make him tough to beat defensively, though his physical edge can sometimes lead to penalties.

name: Erik Karlsson
summary: Erik Karlsson remains one of the league’s most gifted offensive defensemen. His skating and puck movement drive play, even if his high-risk style occasionally results in turnovers. When he’s on, he transforms the Penguins’ transition game into a weapon.

name: Tyson Jost
summary: Tyson Jost brings energy and structure to Carolina’s lineup. He plays well within the team’s fast, possession-heavy system, helping drive play both on and off the puck.

name: John Beecher
summary: John Beecher brings size and defensive responsibility to Boston’s depth chart. He blocks shots and plays a hard-nosed style that fits well with the Bruins’ identity.

name: Dylan Larkin
summary: Dylan Larkin continues to be Detroit’s engine. His speed and drive push the pace, and he’s dangerous off the rush. He’s also strong defensively and reliable in big moments, embodying the modern two-way center role.

name: Cam Fowler
summary: Cam Fowler is a smooth-skating defenseman who moves the puck efficiently. His passing helps transition play up ice, and he remains calm under pressure in his own zone.

name: Matthew Knies
summary: Matthew Knies has shown great poise for a young player. He battles hard in front of the net, scoring off rebounds and winning puck battles. His physicality and net-front presence already make him a valuable asset for Toronto.

name: Artemi Panarin
summary: Artemi Panarin is an offensive artist. His creativity and puck control generate constant scoring chances, whether shooting or setting up teammates. He’s one of the league’s best playmakers and a key reason for New York’s offensive success.

name: Warren Foegele
summary: Warren Foegele is a high-energy winger who thrives on forechecking and driving to the net. He generates second-chance opportunities with his effort and speed, making him a dependable depth scorer.

name: Marcus Pettersson
summary: Marcus Pettersson quietly does his job on the back end. He moves the puck efficiently and supports the rush when needed, providing steady play for Vancouver’s defense group.

name: Adrian Kempe
summary: Adrian Kempe has become one of Los Angeles’s most reliable scorers. His quick release and skating make him a threat from anywhere, and his playmaking complements his goal-scoring perfectly.

name: MacKenzie Weegar
summary: MacKenzie Weegar brings a mix of toughness and offense to Calgary’s blue line. He blocks shots, hits hard, and isn’t afraid to jump into the rush. While he can be turnover-prone, his energy and impact are undeniable.

name: Travis Konecny
summary: Travis Konecny is the emotional leader of the Flyers. He plays with fire, scoring goals and setting up teammates while never shying away from contact. His aggressiveness fuels Philadelphia’s attack.

name: Dylan Strome
summary: Dylan Strome has quietly become one of Washington’s most reliable playmakers. He creates chances with his passing and patience and has found consistency as a top-six center.

name: Josh Morrissey
summary: Josh Morrissey is the backbone of Winnipeg’s defense. He contributes heavily on offense with crisp passing and smart shooting while maintaining strong defensive positioning. His all-around play continues to shine.

name: Mackie Samoskevich
summary: Mackie Samoskevich plays a mature, possession-focused game. His strong control of the puck and ability to drive play up ice make him an exciting young player for Florida.

name: Dmitry Orlov
summary: Dmitry Orlov brings stability and smart decision-making to Carolina’s blue line. He’s excellent in transition, helping his team move the puck efficiently while staying reliable defensively.

name: Devon Toews
summary: Devon Toews is one of the NHL’s most dependable defensemen. He complements Colorado’s high-paced style perfectly, using smart positioning and clean puck movement to keep play flowing smoothly.

name: Brandon Tanev
summary: Brandon Tanev is a relentless competitor who blocks shots, throws hits, and never stops skating. His energy and hustle make him a fan favorite and a nightmare for opposing forwards.

name: Juha Jaaska
summary: Juha Jaaska has shown strong puck possession and awareness in limited ice time. He fits Carolina’s fast-paced, structured system and could grow into a dependable depth winger.

name: Timo Meier
summary: Timo Meier is a power forward who blends physicality with scoring touch. He’s unafraid to shoot, drives the net hard, and consistently generates dangerous scoring chances for New Jersey.

name: Nate Schmidt
summary: Nate Schmidt is a smooth-skating defenseman who excels in puck movement and maintaining offensive pressure. His reliable play makes him a strong complementary piece on the back end.

name: Bryan Rust
summary: Bryan Rust brings speed, effort, and timely scoring to the Penguins. He’s strong around the net, creates rebounds, and plays with the kind of energy that drives Pittsburgh’s attack.

name: Lucas Raymond
summary: Lucas Raymond continues to develop into a creative offensive force. His quick decision-making and vision allow him to set up teammates and generate offense in bunches.

name: Darren Raddysh
summary: Darren Raddysh has quietly become a dependable puck-mover for Tampa Bay. He reads the game well, makes crisp outlet passes, and contributes solid defensive shifts.

name: Matt Coronato
summary: Matt Coronato is a hardworking young winger who isn’t afraid to get in shooting lanes or go to tough areas. His commitment to defense and effort off the puck are promising signs.

name: Adam Lowry
summary: Adam Lowry is a steady two-way center who brings leadership and defensive reliability. He wins battles, kills penalties, and brings a calm, stabilizing presence down the middle.

name: Andrew Mangiapane
summary: Andrew Mangiapane is a versatile middle-six winger who can move up and down the lineup as needed. His significant time on the bench indicates a role where his ice time fluctuates based on game situation and team performance.

name: Teuvo Teravainen
summary: Teuvo Teräväinen is a highly intelligent and skilled playmaker. His value is driven by his elite vision and passing ability, consistently ranking among the league's top forwards in setting up his teammates for goals.

name: J.T. Miller
summary: J.T. Miller is a high-event player who blends elite playmaking with a rugged, physical style. He is a premier setup man and point producer who plays a hard-hitting, agitating game that frequently results in penalties both for and against.

name: Eeli Tolvanen
summary: Eeli Tolvanen is a hard-nosed winger who defines his game with physicality. He is one of the league's most frequent hitters and complements his punishing style with a strong willingness to block shots.

name: John Marino
summary: John Marino is a smooth-skating, reliable defenseman who contributes to a strong blue line. His team's excellent performance in expected goals when he is off the ice highlights the overall depth and quality of the defensive corps.

name: Anders Lee
summary: Anders Lee is one of the most dominant and effective net-front presences in the entire league. Virtually all of his offense is generated from the toughest areas of the ice, where he excels at creating rebounds, getting shots from the slot, and converting second-chance opportunities.

name: Jake Neighbours
summary: Jake Neighbours is an energetic and agitating forward who thrives on physical engagement. He is a frequent hitter who excels at getting under the opponent's skin, resulting in him taking and drawing a high number of penalties.

name: Erik Gustafsson
summary: Erik Gustafsson is an effective puck-moving defenseman who helps tilt the ice in his team's favor. When he is on the ice, his team consistently controls the flow of play, generating a higher quality and quantity of shot attempts than they allow.

name: Michael Pezzetta
summary: Michael Pezzetta is a high-energy fourth-line player who makes a surprisingly strong impact in his limited minutes. When deployed, his line is exceptionally effective at controlling the quality of scoring chances.

name: Casey Mittelstadt
summary: Casey Mittelstadt is a skilled offensive center whose creativity can be a double-edged sword. While he is very effective at cleaning up rebounds around the net, his high-risk style leads to a lot of turnovers and goals against.

name: Tye Kartye
summary: Tye Kartye is a physical fourth-line winger who brings energy every shift. His primary contribution to the team is his relentless, high-volume hitting.

name: Nino Niederreiter
summary: Nino Niederreiter is a skilled middle-six winger with a knack for getting to the front of the net. He excels at generating shots from prime scoring areas, providing valuable offense in a supporting role.

name: Alex Tuch
summary: Alex Tuch is a rare breed of power forward who is just as dominant defensively as he is offensively. He is an elite goal scorer who also ranks at the very top of the league for his position in both takeaways and blocked shots, making him a uniquely effective two-way force.

name: Josh Anderson
summary: Josh Anderson is a classic power forward who uses his combination of size and speed to create chaos. He is an incredibly physical player who is consistently among the league leaders in both taking and drawing penalties.

name: Trevor Lewis
summary: Trevor Lewis is a veteran fourth-line center and a key component of a team that excels at puck possession. The Kings' ability to maintain elite control of the game when he is on the bench speaks to his role as a reliable depth piece in a strong system.

name: Matty Beniers
summary: Matty Beniers is a responsible young center who is already developing a strong two-way game. His standout defensive contribution is his elite willingness to block shots, showcasing a commitment to playing in all three zones.

name: Morgan Rielly
summary: Morgan Rielly is a premier offensive defenseman who consistently drives positive results. He is an excellent playmaker from the back end whose team generates a high rate of quality chances and goals whenever he is on the ice.

name: Jake Middleton
summary: Jake Middleton is a rugged, defense-first blueliner who plays a simple and effective game. His value comes from his physicality, his willingness to block shots, and his ability to agitate opponents into taking penalties.

name: Morgan Geekie
summary: Morgan Geekie has developed into a surprisingly effective two-way threat. He has a special talent for converting rebounds into goals while also contributing defensively with a high rate of takeaways.

name: Dakota Joshua
summary: Dakota Joshua is a hard-charging depth forward who makes his presence felt through physicality. He is a high-volume hitter who brings energy and grit to the bottom six.

name: Jalen Chatfield
summary: Jalen Chatfield is a solid depth defenseman who is a perfect fit for a dominant possession team. The Hurricanes' ability to control the game at a historic rate even when he is on the bench demonstrates the incredible strength of their system and defensive corps.

name: Patrick Kane
summary: Even in the later stages of his career, Patrick Kane remains an elite and creative playmaker. His exceptional vision continues to make him one of the best in the league at finding the open man and contributing to the scoresheet.

name: John Tavares
summary: John Tavares continues to be one of the NHL's most elite net-front scorers. He consistently generates a massive volume of shots from high-danger areas, resulting in prolific goal-scoring numbers and a strong positive impact on his team's offense.

name: Tom Wilson
summary: Tom Wilson is the league's preeminent power forward, a unique force of nature who blends scoring touch with overwhelming physicality. He is an elite agitator who leads the league in drawing and taking penalties, a punishing hitter, and an effective goal scorer from in close.

name: Jamie Oleksiak
summary: Jamie Oleksiak uses his massive frame to serve as a shutdown defenseman in a depth role. He is a high-end shot blocker, providing a steady, defensive-minded presence on the back end.

name: Jordan Kyrou
summary: Jordan Kyrou is a pure goal scorer with a shoot-first, ask-questions-later mentality. He is a high-volume shooter who unleashes the puck from anywhere in the offensive zone, consistently resulting in elite goal totals even if many of his attempts are blocked.

name: Derek Forbort
summary: Derek Forbort is a physical, stay-at-home defenseman whose agitating style is effective at goading opponents into taking penalties.

name: Jordan Staal
summary: As the shutdown center for the league's most dominant possession team, Jordan Staal's off-ice metrics are a testament to his team's incredible depth. The Hurricanes control the game at a historic rate, regardless of whether their top defensive forward is on the ice or resting.

name: Gabriel Vilardi
summary: Gabriel Vilardi is a skilled forward who has a clear positive impact on his team's ability to score. When he is on the ice, his line consistently finds the back of the net.

name: A.J. Greer
summary: A.J. Greer is a quintessential enforcer who plays a highly specialized and limited role. He is one of the league's most penalized and physical players, bringing immense energy and agitation in very short bursts from the fourth line.

name: Lane Hutson
summary: Lane Hutson is a dynamic and supremely talented modern defenseman who impacts the game at both ends of the ice. He is an elite playmaker and point producer from the blue line who also possesses a rare, elite ability to strip opponents of the puck.

name: Mark Stone
summary: Mark Stone is one of the most intelligent and effective two-way wingers in the NHL. He is a truly elite playmaker and arguably the best takeaway artist of his generation, consistently driving play and creating offense through his incredible hockey sense.

name: Conor Geekie
summary: Conor Geekie is a young prospect working his way into a very deep and talented lineup. His team's strong performance when he's not on the ice is a reflection of the organization's overall strength.

name: Rasmus Ristolainen
summary: Rasmus Ristolainen is a big, physical defenseman who can contribute offensively by getting pucks toward the net. He is effective at generating rebounds and second-chance opportunities with his point shots.

name: Ryan Suter
summary: Ryan Suter is a veteran defenseman who has settled into a reduced role later in his career. He now provides steady, experienced depth on the third pairing.

name: Damon Severson
summary: Damon Severson is an offensively aggressive defenseman who loves to activate into the play. He shows an elite ability to get shots off from high-danger areas and the slot, contributing to the offense like a fourth forward.

name: Zach Benson
summary: Zach Benson is a young and tenacious winger who plays a feisty, agitating style. He is constantly involved in the physical game, leading to him both taking and drawing a high number of penalties.

name: Michael Eyssimont
summary: Michael Eyssimont is a fourth-line energy player who brings grit in a limited role. He plays with an edge that frequently lands him in the penalty box.

name: Martin Necas
summary: Martin Nečas is a dynamic, elite-skating forward who dominates the flow of the game. He is an exceptional playmaker and possession driver, using his speed to help his team control the puck and generate offense at a remarkable rate.

name: Ty Dellandrea
summary: Ty Dellandrea is a gritty, bottom-six forward who plays an aggressive game. His physical style often results in him being among the team leaders in penalties taken.

name: Jeremy Lauzon
summary: Jeremy Lauzon is a defense-first blueliner whose game is defined by physicality. He is a high-volume hitter, tasked with clearing the front of the net and punishing opposing forwards.

name: Stefan Noesen
summary: Stefan Noesen is a premier net-front specialist who excels on the power play. He has an elite talent for getting to the most difficult areas of the ice to generate high-danger shots and create rebound opportunities.

name: Logan Cooley
summary: Logan Cooley is a highly skilled young playmaker who is still refining his game. He shows flashes of elite passing ability but also plays with a feistiness that can lead to undisciplined penalties.

name: Samuel Helenius
summary: Samuel Helenius is a young depth prospect for a strong, contending team. The team's excellent performance while he is off the ice highlights the established talent in the main lineup.

name: Josh Manson
summary: Josh Manson is a rugged, veteran defenseman who provides reliable depth for a powerhouse team. His strong off-ice metrics are a testament to Colorado's overall team dominance and the effective role he plays within it.

name: Brent Burns
summary: Brent Burns remains one of the league's most prolific volume-shooting defensemen. He embraces a shoot-from-anywhere mentality, leading to a massive number of total shot attempts, though many are often blocked or miss the net.

name: Esa Lindell
summary: Esa Lindell is a top-tier shutdown defenseman who logs incredibly tough minutes against the opposition's best players. He is an elite shot blocker, consistently sacrificing his body to anchor the team's defensive efforts.

name: Nathan Walker
summary: Nathan Walker is a fourth-line wrecking ball who plays one of the most physical games in the NHL. He is a relentless, high-volume hitter who brings a punishing energy and a willingness to block shots every shift.

name: Connor Bedard
summary: Connor Bedard is a generational talent tasked with carrying the offensive burden for a rebuilding team. He is an elite playmaker who handles the puck constantly, leading to high giveaway totals and tough defensive minutes against top competition as he single-handedly tries to drive the offense.

name: Nic Dowd
summary: Nic Dowd is a defensive specialist who quietly handles tough assignments for Washington. He blocks shots, creates turnovers, and plays a reliable, grind-it-out game that makes him a key penalty killer and depth center.

name: Sam Bennett
summary: Sam Bennett plays an aggressive, high-energy style that perfectly fits Florida’s identity. He drives hard to the net, creates rebounds, and isn’t afraid to get physical. His ability to blend scoring with grit makes him a key part of the Panthers’ attack.

name: Jackson Blake
summary: Jackson Blake has impressed with his poise and control when on the ice. Carolina dominates possession with him out there, thanks to his strong puck movement and smart positioning. His energy and awareness suggest real upside in his young career.

name: Cole Smith
summary: Cole Smith plays a hard-working depth role for Nashville. He may not fill the scoresheet, but his defensive structure and attention to detail help his team in the small moments that win games.

name: Sam Carrick
summary: Sam Carrick brings toughness and a veteran presence to the Rangers’ bottom six. He plays a physical game, finishes checks, and adds sandpaper to New York’s forward group.

name: Pavel Buchnevich
summary: Pavel Buchnevich continues to be one of St. Louis’s most skilled and consistent forwards. His blend of finesse and finish makes him a reliable top-six scoring option capable of changing a game with one play.

name: Casey Cizikas
summary: Casey Cizikas embodies the hard-working identity of the Islanders. He hits, blocks shots, and brings relentless effort every shift. A classic bottom-six center, he thrives on doing the dirty work others avoid.

name: Vincent Trocheck
summary: Vincent Trocheck has been outstanding for New York, combining playmaking skill with hard-nosed effort. He wins battles, draws penalties, and contributes in all situations. His energy and two-way play have made him one of the Rangers’ most valuable players.

name: Artturi Lehkonen
summary: Artturi Lehkonen is a relentless worker who excels around the net. He scores the tough goals in traffic and plays smart, responsible hockey on both ends, making him an essential part of Colorado’s balanced attack.

name: Yakov Trenin
summary: Yakov Trenin is a physical forward who uses his size to wear down opponents. He throws his body around and brings a grinding style that fits Minnesota’s blue-collar identity.

name: Zach Bogosian
summary: Zach Bogosian is a stay-at-home defenseman who brings leadership and toughness. He keeps things simple in his own end, providing a calming veteran presence for the Wild.

name: Ryan McLeod
summary: Ryan McLeod is a smooth-skating center who helps drive play with his speed. He supports defensively and chips in offensively, adding reliable depth to Buffalo’s lineup.

name: Andrei Kuzmenko
summary: Andrei Kuzmenko adds a touch of skill and creativity to Los Angeles’s forward core. He’s strong in puck possession and helps his team control play whenever he’s on the ice.

name: Matt Duchene
summary: Matt Duchene remains an elite offensive talent with smooth hands and high hockey IQ. He consistently produces points and creates space for his linemates, giving Dallas veteran scoring depth down the middle.

name: Cody Ceci
summary: Cody Ceci provides steady, physical defense for Dallas. He blocks plenty of shots, plays big minutes, and stays reliable in his own zone, giving the Stars consistency on the back end.

name: Vladislav Gavrikov
summary: Vladislav Gavrikov is a reliable stay-at-home defenseman who brings a calm, structured game. He rarely makes mistakes and helps stabilize the Kings’ blue line.

name: Tommy Novak
summary: Tommy Novak plays a patient, smart game in the middle of the ice. He contributes to sustained puck possession and moves the play in the right direction for Pittsburgh.

name: Nathan MacKinnon
summary: Nathan MacKinnon is a superstar in every sense. His explosive speed, elite shot, and relentless drive make him nearly unstoppable. Whether creating for teammates or finishing himself, he’s the heartbeat of Colorado’s offense.

name: Elias Pettersson
summary: Elias Pettersson combines high-end skill with surprising defensive effort. He reads plays well, contributes in all zones, and continues to grow as one of Vancouver’s most complete players.

name: Pontus Holmberg
summary: Pontus Holmberg plays with great effort and isn’t afraid to draw penalties with his aggressive style. His hustle and tenacity give Toronto valuable depth on the wings.

name: Mitch Marner
summary: Mitch Marner is a magician with the puck. His playmaking, vision, and defensive awareness make him one of the league’s most complete wingers. He drives Toronto’s offense and creates chances out of nothing.

name: Zach Whitecloud
summary: Zach Whitecloud is a dependable defender who supports his team’s structure well. He plays simple, mistake-free hockey and contributes quietly to Vegas’s defensive strength.

name: Bo Horvat
summary: Bo Horvat is a consistent offensive force with a powerful shot and net-front presence. He thrives in traffic, winning puck battles and creating second-chance opportunities for the Islanders.

name: Justin Dowling
summary: Justin Dowling adds veteran depth and experience to New Jersey’s roster. He may not play heavy minutes, but he brings reliability when called upon.

name: Brady Skjei
summary: Brady Skjei is a smooth, mobile defenseman who contributes at both ends of the ice. He moves the puck efficiently and chips in offensively while holding his own defensively.

name: Will Borgen
summary: Will Borgen provides a big, physical presence on defense. He plays a stay-at-home style and keeps opponents honest with his strength and positioning.

name: David Gustafsson
summary: David Gustafsson is a responsible two-way center who plays within the system. His awareness and effort make him a dependable depth option for the Jets.

name: Justin Faulk
summary: Justin Faulk is a steady, veteran defenseman who contributes offensively with a quick shot and smart pinches. His experience helps anchor St. Louis’s blue line.

name: Shea Theodore
summary: Shea Theodore is an elite puck-moving defenseman who thrives in transition. His skating and vision drive Vegas’s offense from the back end, and he’s equally capable of shutting down top lines.

name: Filip Forsberg
summary: Filip Forsberg is Nashville’s offensive engine. His creativity and shot-making ability make him dangerous from anywhere on the ice, and his compete level never dips. He’s the go-to finisher for the Predators.

name: Tage Thompson
summary: Tage Thompson is a towering goal scorer with a lethal shot. His reach and quick release make him nearly impossible to defend, and he continues to be Buffalo’s most explosive offensive weapon.

name: Nazem Kadri
summary: Nazem Kadri brings fire and flair to Calgary’s lineup. He’s aggressive on the puck, drives the offense, and draws plenty of penalties. His energy and competitiveness make him a tone-setter for the Flames.

name: Martin Pospisil
summary: Martin Pospisil plays a bruising, in-your-face style. He racks up hits and gets under opponents’ skin, providing a spark for Calgary whenever he’s on the ice.

name: Ryan McDonagh
summary: Ryan McDonagh remains a defensive workhorse for Tampa Bay. He sacrifices his body to block shots and plays heavy minutes against top competition, continuing to lead by example.

name: Ryan Strome
summary: Ryan Strome brings grit and leadership to a young Anaheim team. He plays with an edge, isn’t afraid to mix it up, and provides secondary scoring from the middle.

name: Brayden Point
summary: Brayden Point is one of the NHL’s most efficient goal scorers. His quick release, speed, and hockey sense make him a constant threat, and he remains the perfect complement to Tampa’s high-powered offense.

name: Garnet Hathaway
summary: Garnet Hathaway is a throwback-style player who plays every shift like it’s his last. He hits everything that moves, blocks shots, and adds intensity and leadership to Philadelphia’s bottom six.

name: Cutter Gauthier
summary: Cutter Gauthier brings energy and offensive upside to Anaheim’s rebuild. He fires pucks from everywhere and isn’t afraid to battle in front, showing promise as a future top-line winger.

name: Joel Kiviranta
summary: Joel Kiviranta is a hardworking depth winger who brings speed and responsibility. He supports his team well defensively and helps drive positive possession when on the ice.

name: Anthony Beauvillier
summary: Anthony Beauvillier is a quick, versatile forward who brings speed and energy to Washington’s lineup. His ability to play up and down the roster adds flexibility to the team’s forward depth.

name: Rasmus Dahlin
summary: Rasmus Dahlin is a game-changing defenseman. He controls the pace of play, contributes heavily on offense, and has become one of the league’s most complete blueliners. His blend of vision, skill, and confidence makes him Buffalo’s cornerstone.

name: Macklin Celebrini
summary: Macklin Celebrini shows why he’s one of hockey’s brightest young talents. His creativity, skating, and willingness to shoot make him the centerpiece of San Jose’s rebuild.

name: Nick DeSimone
summary: Nick DeSimone brings stability to Utah’s defense corps. He plays within the system, keeps play moving up ice, and provides dependable minutes when called upon.

name: Kyle Connor
summary: Kyle Connor is one of the NHL’s purest goal scorers. His quick hands and elite release allow him to score from anywhere, and he continues to lead Winnipeg’s offense with consistency and flair.

name: Vince Dunn
summary: Vince Dunn is a reliable puck-moving defenseman who adds an offensive spark from the back end. His ability to join the rush and handle pressure makes him a valuable piece of Seattle’s core.

name: Corey Perry
summary: Corey Perry brings experience, toughness, and a little mischief to every shift. He’s still effective around the crease, drawing penalties and frustrating opponents with his veteran savvy.

name: Zachary Aston-Reese
summary: Zachary Aston-Reese is a physical depth forward who plays a hard, north-south game. He’s dependable defensively and always finishes his checks.

name: Brandon Carlo
summary: Brandon Carlo is a steady defensive presence known for his size and shot-blocking. He doesn’t overcomplicate things and provides Toronto with reliable minutes in shutdown situations.

name: Jean-Gabriel Pageau
summary: Jean-Gabriel Pageau is a reliable two-way center who brings energy and responsibility to the Islanders’ lineup. He excels in defensive situations and chips in timely goals.

name: Quinton Byfield
summary: Quinton Byfield continues to grow into a dynamic power forward. His size, speed, and playmaking ability make him a matchup problem, and he’s learning to blend physicality with skill at the NHL level.

name: Claude Giroux
summary: Claude Giroux remains a skilled veteran forward who is trusted to play in all situations. He is often deployed in difficult matchups against top opposition, which results in his line being on the ice for a high rate of quality chances and goals against.

name: Carl Grundstrom
summary: Carl Grundström is a fourth-line energy player whose game is built around physicality. He makes his presence felt with a high volume of hits.

name: William Nylander
summary: William Nylander is a truly elite and dynamic offensive force. He is one of the league's top goal scorers, a prolific point producer, and a high-volume shooter who consistently drives offensive chances and goals for his team whenever he is on the ice.

name: Mike Reilly
summary: Mike Reilly is an exceptionally effective possession-driving defenseman. When he is on the ice, his team controls the flow of play at an elite level, dominating the quantity and quality of shot attempts.

name: Isac Lundestrom
summary: Isac Lundeström is a defense-first center who contributes by doing the little things right. His most valuable asset is his willingness to get in front of pucks, ranking among the best shot-blocking forwards in the league.

name: Oliver Ekman-Larsson
summary: Oliver Ekman-Larsson is a veteran defenseman who plays with a physical edge. His aggressive style can sometimes cross the line, resulting in a high number of penalties taken.

name: Ryan O'Reilly
summary: Ryan O'Reilly is the quintessential master-of-all-trades two-way center. He excels in every facet of the game, getting to the high-danger areas to score, blocking shots like a defenseman, stripping opponents of the puck, and consistently driving possession for his line.

name: Nico Hischier
summary: Nico Hischier is an elite offensive center who creates goals through sheer will and skill. He is one of the league's best at generating shots from the most dangerous areas of the ice, converting rebounds, and using his tenacity to draw a high number of penalties.

name: Jayden Struble
summary: Jayden Struble is a young, physical defenseman who is still carving out his role. He brings a physical dimension to the blue line with a consistent willingness to throw the body.

name: William Carrier
summary: William Carrier is a perfect cog in a dominant machine. He is a possession-driving fourth liner who helps his team control the puck when he's on the ice, and his excellent off-ice metrics show the team continues that dominance with its other lines.

name: Ethen Frank
summary: Ethen Frank is a depth forward who has shown the ability to make a positive impact in limited action. When he is on the ice, his line does a great job of controlling the quality of scoring chances.

name: Nick Seeler
summary: Nick Seeler is a pure, old-school defensive defenseman who specializes in protecting his own net. He is an elite and fearless shot blocker who complements his shutdown style with a punishing, physical game.

name: Brett Kulak
summary: Brett Kulak is a reliable third-pairing defenseman on a very strong team. Edmonton's ability to maintain excellent puck possession when he's on the bench is a testament to his effective role-playing and the team's overall depth.

name: Vinnie Hinostroza
summary: Vinnie Hinostroza is a skilled depth forward who makes a significant impact on scoring chances. In his minutes, his line is exceptionally good at creating higher quality opportunities than they give up.

name: Cole Perfetti
summary: Cole Perfetti is a smart young forward who helps tilt the ice in a positive direction. When he is on the ice, his line consistently generates a better quality of scoring chances than the opposition.

name: Chris Kreider
summary: Chris Kreider continues to be one of the league's most effective net-front presences. His game is defined by using his size and speed to get to the most dangerous areas of the ice to generate scoring chances.

name: Gustav Forsling
summary: Gustav Forsling has blossomed into a premier two-way defenseman on a dominant team. He is a high-volume shooter with a goal-scorer's touch from the point and is also excellent defensively at creating takeaways.

name: Dylan McIlrath
summary: Dylan McIlrath is a veteran, physical defenseman who provides organizational depth. The team's strong performance when he is off the ice reflects his role as a situational, seventh defenseman.

name: Lawson Crouse
summary: Lawson Crouse is a prototypical power forward who plays a heavy game in a middle-six role. His primary contribution is his consistent, high-volume physical play.

name: Vladislav Namestnikov
summary: Vladislav Namestnikov is a versatile veteran forward who contributes as a complementary playmaker. He effectively helps facilitate offense for his linemates, chipping in consistently on the scoresheet.

name: Mattias Janmark
summary: Mattias Janmark is a dependable fourth-line penalty killer on an offensive juggernaut. His historic off-ice metrics are a clear reflection of the overwhelming dominance of Edmonton's superstar-led top lines.

name: Olli Mtt
summary: Olli Määttä is a veteran defenseman who can still contribute offensively by jumping into the play. He shows a strong ability to find open space and get shots off from the slot.

name: Nick Perbix
summary: Nick Perbix is a young defenseman who has earned a regular spot on the third pairing. He plays a steady, if limited, role for the team.

name: Braden Schneider
summary: Braden Schneider is a young, physical defenseman who plays a depth role. He is a high-volume hitter who provides energy and toughness on the third pairing.

name: Uvis Balinskis
summary: Uvis Balinskis is a depth defenseman who has shown he can drive possession in limited minutes. He serves as reliable injury insurance for a deep blue line.

name: Adam Boqvist
summary: Adam Boqvist is a skilled, offensive-minded defenseman who consistently helps his team control the game. When he is on the ice, his team generates more shots and higher-quality chances than they allow.

name: John Carlson
summary: John Carlson is a premier offensive defenseman and a high-event workhorse. He is an elite playmaker from the blue line who drives the creation of a massive number of goals and quality chances, though his high-risk style can also lead to turnovers.

name: Troy Terry
summary: Troy Terry is a skilled and crafty forward who excels at creating offense in multiple ways. He is a strong playmaker who is particularly adept at drawing penalties and creating second-chance opportunities with his shots.

name: Thomas Chabot
summary: Thomas Chabot is a minute-munching, offense-first defenseman. He is a high-volume shooter who loves to activate into the play and is a key playmaker from the back end, though many of his shot attempts get blocked.

name: Conor Garland
summary: Conor Garland is one of the league's elite pests, a highly effective agitator who drives opponents to distraction. His greatest skill is his ability to draw penalties, which he complements with a knack for scoring goals on rebounds.

name: Brad Marchand
summary: Brad Marchand is the NHL's ultimate agitator, blending elite offensive talent with a uniquely irritating style. He is a master at both drawing and taking penalties, while also being highly effective at getting to the front of the net to generate high-danger scoring chances.

name: Sebastian Aho
summary: Sebastian Aho is an elite two-way center who drives play at a historic rate. He is a masterful playmaker who helps his team dominate puck possession, though he is also a high-volume shooter who misses the net more than almost anyone.

name: Tyler Bertuzzi
summary: Tyler Bertuzzi is a gritty scoring winger who makes his living in the tough areas of the ice. He excels at generating shots from right in front of the net but is often deployed in high-event situations that lead to goals at both ends of the rink.

name: Miro Heiskanen
summary: Miro Heiskanen is a truly elite all-around defenseman who has a profound impact on the game. He is a dominant possession driver who ensures his team controls the quality and quantity of shots whenever he is on the ice.

name: Anton Lundell
summary: Anton Lundell is a responsible, defensively-sound center who plays a key middle-six role on a powerhouse team. His excellent off-ice metrics are a clear indicator of Florida's incredible depth and its ability to control play with all four lines.

name: Nick Schmaltz
summary: Nick Schmaltz is a highly creative but high-risk offensive player. His line generates an elite rate of quality scoring chances, but his playmaking style often leads to him being among the league leaders in turnovers.

name: Taylor Hall
summary: Though no longer the primary offensive driver, Taylor Hall has adapted his game to contribute defensively. He remains one of the best wingers in the league at stripping opponents of the puck.

name: Drew O'Connor
summary: Drew O'Connor is an effective defensive specialist who excels in a fourth-line role. In his limited minutes, he is elite at creating takeaways and providing responsible defensive play.

name: Matthew Tkachuk
summary: Matthew Tkachuk is one of the most unique and dominant players in the NHL. He is a truly generational possession driver who controls the game like few others, combining his puck dominance with an elite ability to create chaos and scoring chances around the net.

name: Dylan DeMelo
summary: Dylan DeMelo is a reliable, defense-oriented blueliner. He contributes by playing a physical game and has a knack for agitating opponents into taking penalties.

name: Andrei Svechnikov
summary: Andrei Svechnikov is a chaotic and thrilling power forward who is the embodiment of high-event hockey. He is an elite possession driver who plays a reckless, aggressive style that leads to a very high number of both turnovers and penalties.

name: Noel Acciari
summary: Noel Acciari is a premier defensive fourth-liner who excels at the thankless parts of the game. He is one of the most fearless and prolific shot blockers among forwards and complements it with a punishing, high-volume hitting style.

name: Teddy Blueger
summary: Teddy Blueger is a veteran fourth-line center who is relied upon for his defensive acumen. He plays a limited but steady role, primarily focused on penalty killing and defensive zone starts.

name: Jonah Gadjovich
summary: Jonah Gadjovich is a fourth-line enforcer who provides energy and toughness. He plays a limited role on a very deep team that controls possession with all of its lines.

name: Drake Batherson
summary: Drake Batherson is a skilled top-six winger who creates offense in multiple ways. He is an excellent playmaker and is also highly effective at generating quality scoring chances for himself from the slot.

name: Cole Caufield
summary: Cole Caufield is a pure, elite sniper. His entire offensive game is built around a lightning-quick release and an innate ability to find soft spots in the defense, resulting in a massive volume of shots from the slot and prolific goal-scoring totals.

name: Alex Iafallo
summary: Alex Iafallo is a dependable two-way winger who contributes in a variety of ways. He is effective at cleaning up loose pucks around the net and is also a willing shot blocker in the defensive zone.

name: Logan O'Connor
summary: Logan O'Connor is a speedy, high-energy fourth liner who excels at his role. His tenacity on the forecheck makes him very effective at drawing penalties from opposing defensemen.

name: Fabian Zetterlund
summary: Fabian Zetterlund is a unique two-way winger with a rare and valuable skillset. He is elite among forwards at blocking shots while also possessing a special knack for scoring goals on rebounds around the net.

name: Brock Nelson
summary: Brock Nelson is a highly effective two-way center who excels at disrupting the opposition. He is one of the absolute best forwards in the league at creating takeaways, a skill which helps his line limit the quality of chances against.

name: Mikhail Sergachev
summary: Mikhail Sergachev is a dynamic defenseman who thrives in transition and drives offense from the blue line. He logs heavy minutes, contributes goals and assists, and isn’t afraid to jump into the play, though his risk-taking can sometimes lead to giveaways.

name: Luke Kunin
summary: Luke Kunin brings a mix of grit and responsibility to Columbus’s forward group. He plays hard, blocks shots, throws his weight around, and gives his team a physical edge while staying defensively sound.

name: Erik Cernak
summary: Erik Cernak is a tough, reliable defender who uses his size and physical play to protect Tampa Bay’s net. He’s steady in his own end and brings the kind of edge every team needs in the playoffs.

name: Jamie Benn
summary: Jamie Benn continues to play his trademark power-forward game. He mixes physicality with scoring touch and isn’t shy about playing on the edge to spark his team.

name: Sam Malinski
summary: Sam Malinski plays a composed, steady game from the back end. He keeps plays simple, moves the puck efficiently, and provides depth support to Colorado’s defensive core.

name: Hendrix Lapierre
summary: Hendrix Lapierre is a young center with strong offensive instincts. He reads the ice well and helps his team create chances when he’s on the ice, showing promise as he earns more experience.

name: Keegan Kolesar
summary: Keegan Kolesar is a bruising winger who plays a hard-nosed, north-south game. He’s relentless on the forecheck, finishes checks with authority, and brings energy to every shift.

name: Jonathan Marchessault
summary: Jonathan Marchessault remains a dangerous shooter with a knack for finding space. He gets pucks on net from all angles and adds a spark of offense to Nashville’s lineup.

name: Aaron Ekblad
summary: Aaron Ekblad is a cornerstone of Florida’s defense. He plays heavy minutes in all situations, balances physical play with smart puck movement, and can tilt the ice both offensively and defensively.

name: Nikita Zadorov
summary: Nikita Zadorov is one of the league’s most intimidating physical defensemen. He hits hard, plays with an edge, and keeps opponents honest while adding grit and toughness to Boston’s blue line.

name: Dmitri Voronkov
summary: Dmitri Voronkov is a powerful forward who excels in tight spaces. He crashes the net, creates second-chance opportunities, and scores his goals from hard-working areas.

name: Jaccob Slavin
summary: Jaccob Slavin is one of the NHL’s most polished defenders. He’s calm under pressure, rarely makes mistakes, and drives possession with his smart stick and smooth skating. His defensive instincts are elite.

name: Cale Makar
summary: Cale Makar is a generational defenseman who can take over a game. His skating, vision, and offensive creativity make him Colorado’s engine from the back end, and he pairs it with elite defensive reads.

name: Brayden Pachal
summary: Brayden Pachal brings toughness and effort to Calgary’s defense. He plays with an edge, blocks shots, and keeps opponents uncomfortable with his physical play.

name: Will Cuylle
summary: Will Cuylle is a power forward who loves to throw his body around. He’s fearless near the crease, scoring gritty goals and adding toughness to the Rangers’ lineup.

name: Noah Hanifin
summary: Noah Hanifin is a steady, mobile defenseman who plays big minutes for Vegas. He contributes on both ends of the ice with confident puck movement and reliable two-way play.

name: Alexandre Carrier
summary: Alexandre Carrier is a solid defensive defenseman who sacrifices his body for the team. His shot-blocking and discipline make him a trusted presence on Montreal’s back end.

name: Sam Reinhart
summary: Sam Reinhart has emerged as one of the league’s most efficient finishers. He combines strong positioning with soft hands, consistently scoring from high-danger areas and driving Florida’s offense.

name: Rasmus Sandin
summary: Rasmus Sandin plays a smart, puck-moving game for Washington. He reads plays well and contributes quietly but effectively to his team’s transition game.

name: Jakob Chychrun
summary: Jakob Chychrun is a modern two-way defenseman with an elite shot from the point. He contributes heavily on offense while still taking care of his own zone with strength and mobility.

name: Taylor Raddysh
summary: Taylor Raddysh provides solid depth for Washington with a hardworking style. He plays responsibly and helps drive energy from the lower lines.

name: Matt Grzelcyk
summary: Matt Grzelcyk is a quick, puck-moving defenseman who supports the rush well. His passing and offensive reads make him a useful piece for generating chances from the blue line.

name: Brandon Montour
summary: Brandon Montour is an aggressive, offensive-minded defenseman who loves to shoot. He jumps into the play, creates scoring chances, and brings high energy to Seattle’s attack.

name: Joel Eriksson Ek
summary: Joel Eriksson Ek is a net-front warrior who thrives on rebounds and chaos. He plays heavy minutes, wins faceoffs, and brings physical edge and consistency to Minnesota’s lineup.

name: Blake Coleman
summary: Blake Coleman is a two-way grinder who does it all for Calgary. He wins puck battles, hits hard, blocks shots, and chips in timely offense when needed.

name: Charlie Coyle
summary: Charlie Coyle plays a strong two-way game built on size and puck protection. He wins battles, drives possession, and helps control the pace for Colorado.

name: Brady Tkachuk
summary: Brady Tkachuk is the heart and soul of Ottawa’s team. He’s fearless in front of the net, plays physically, scores in bunches, and isn’t afraid to drag his team into the fight when needed.

name: Valeri Nichushkin
summary: Valeri Nichushkin is a big, powerful winger who thrives around the crease. He creates chaos for defenders with his forechecking and knack for converting rebounds.

name: Jaden Schwartz
summary: Jaden Schwartz brings leadership and tenacity to Seattle. He’s aggressive on the puck, creates offense through hard work, and adds veteran stability to the Kraken lineup.

name: Jeff Skinner
summary: Jeff Skinner remains a creative goal scorer with quick hands and good instincts. He finds soft spots in coverage and continues to produce offense efficiently for Edmonton.

name: David Savard
summary: David Savard is a stay-at-home defenseman who blocks shots at an elite rate. He provides leadership, toughness, and stability for Montreal’s younger defenders.

name: Mark Jankowski
summary: Mark Jankowski plays a quiet but reliable game. He helps his team control possession and supports defensively with smart positioning and work ethic.

name: Patrick Maroon
summary: Patrick Maroon brings size, toughness, and championship experience to Chicago. He’s physical around the net and provides valuable leadership to a young roster.

name: Morgan Frost
summary: Morgan Frost is a creative playmaker with strong puck skills. He’s confident with the puck and helps generate scoring chances through his vision and quick hands.

name: Noah Philp
summary: Noah Philp contributes to positive puck control whenever he’s on the ice. His positioning and work rate make him a smart, dependable presence down the middle.

name: Jake Evans
summary: Jake Evans is a hardworking center who does the little things right. He’s responsible defensively, draws penalties, and contributes steady two-way play for Montreal.

name: Miles Wood
summary: Miles Wood brings speed and physicality to Colorado’s lineup. He’s aggressive on the forecheck, disruptive in transition, and gives his team momentum with his energy.

name: Arber Xhekaj
summary: Arber Xhekaj is a fan favorite for his toughness and physicality. He throws massive hits, drops the gloves when needed, and brings a fearless edge to Montreal’s blue line.

name: Frederick Gaudreau
summary: Frederick Gaudreau is a defensively aware forward who blocks shots and helps kill penalties. He provides quiet reliability and versatility to Minnesota’s bottom six.

name: Brenden Dillon
summary: Brenden Dillon is a physical, shutdown defenseman who plays with an edge. He punishes opponents along the boards and provides veteran leadership on the Devils’ blue line.

name: Derek Ryan
summary: Derek Ryan is a steady veteran who helps drive play when he’s out there. He’s smart positionally, makes good reads, and supports both ends of the ice efficiently.

name: Troy Stecher
summary: Troy Stecher provides reliable depth on defense for Edmonton. He plays within his limits, moves the puck well, and brings experience to the third pairing.

name: Dawson Mercer
summary: Dawson Mercer is a talented young forward with a quick release and strong work ethic. He drives play toward the net and continues to grow as an offensive threat.

name: Barrett Hayton
summary: Barrett Hayton is a strong two-way center who isn’t afraid to get to the dirty areas. He creates high-quality chances, battles hard for rebounds, and gives Utah a balanced presence up front.

name: Wyatt Johnston
summary: Wyatt Johnston is one of the league’s top young scorers. He combines speed and finishing touch with relentless effort, consistently generating offense for Dallas from the middle.

name: Alexey Toropchenko
summary: Alexey Toropchenko is a big, physical winger who brings energy every night. He hits hard, forechecks aggressively, and gives St. Louis a rugged presence up front.

name: Robert Bortuzzo
summary: Robert Bortuzzo is a stay-at-home veteran who brings toughness and defensive discipline. He keeps his game simple and makes life difficult for opposing forwards.

name: Zach Hyman
summary: Zach Hyman has become one of the NHL’s premier net-front forwards. He scores gritty goals, battles through defenders, and brings nonstop effort that perfectly complements Edmonton’s stars.

name: Jacob Trouba
summary: Jacob Trouba is a hard-hitting, shot-blocking leader who anchors his team’s defense. He plays heavy minutes, sets a physical tone, and leads by example every night.

name: Jack Drury
