summary: Jack Drury provides dependable two-way play and helps tilt the ice in his team’s favor. He’s responsible, efficient, and fits seamlessly into Colorado’s structured system.

name: Ryan Hartman
summary: Ryan Hartman plays a gritty and emotional game that constantly keeps opponents off balance. He draws plenty of penalties by agitating defenders but also takes his fair share, showing his willingness to battle in the hard areas of the ice.

name: Brandon Hagel
summary: Brandon Hagel has developed into one of Tampa Bay’s most complete forwards, thriving in both goal scoring and playmaking roles. His high-end production and relentless effort make him a key driver of offense on one of the league’s most dangerous teams.

name: Ryan Nugent-Hopkins
summary: Ryan Nugent-Hopkins continues to be a cerebral playmaker who excels in tight spaces. His ability to generate dangerous chances and disrupt plays defensively highlights his value as a reliable two-way center for Edmonton.

name: Connor McDavid
summary: Connor McDavid once again proved why he’s the best player in the world. His blazing speed, elite vision, and playmaking instincts drive nearly every scoring chance Edmonton generates, making him unstoppable when he has the puck.

name: Simon Benoit
summary: Simon Benoit plays a rugged defensive style built on physicality and aggression. He punishes opponents with heavy hits and provides steady depth minutes, bringing a tough, stay-at-home presence to Toronto’s blue line.

name: Nicolas Hague
summary: Nicolas Hague quietly anchors Vegas’s defense with poised, efficient play. His team performs better with him off the ice statistically, but his steady positioning and puck-moving ability add balance to the Golden Knights’ back end.

name: Luke Glendening
summary: Luke Glendening is a dependable veteran who brings effort and structure every shift. Though not flashy, his defensive reliability and work ethic make him a trusted bottom-six forward and penalty killer.

name: Trevor van Riemsdyk
summary: Trevor van Riemsdyk plays a low-event defensive game, doing the dirty work in his own zone. He sacrifices his body to block shots and provides quiet stability to Washington’s defense.

name: Tyson Foerster
summary: Tyson Foerster has a knack for generating second-chance opportunities around the net. His tenacity and quick release make him a valuable young scorer in Philadelphia’s offense.

name: Lian Bichsel
summary: Lian Bichsel brings size and strength to Dallas’s defense. His physical play and willingness to engage opponents make him a hard player to face in the corners and in front of the net.

name: Tanner Pearson
summary: Tanner Pearson’s veteran presence provides a steadying influence for Vegas. While not driving offense at his peak levels, his experience and smart play without the puck make him a useful depth piece.

name: Tyler Toffoli
summary: Tyler Toffoli continues to be a consistent scoring threat with his quick shot and knack for finding soft spots in coverage. Even on a rebuilding team, his finishing ability remains elite.

name: Travis Sanheim
summary: Travis Sanheim has taken on major minutes for Philadelphia, showing strong defensive commitment despite occasional risky plays with the puck. His ability to block shots and push play forward highlights his value as an all-around defender.

name: Mikael Backlund
summary: Mikael Backlund remains one of the league’s most underrated two-way centers. His defensive instincts and strong stick make him reliable in shutting down top lines while contributing steady offense.

name: Ridly Greig
summary: Ridly Greig brings a fiery edge to Ottawa’s lineup. He plays with high energy and isn’t afraid to stir things up, providing spark and physical bite to the team’s forward group.

name: Jake DeBrusk
summary: Jake DeBrusk thrives by attacking the net and creating scoring chances from dangerous areas. His speed and willingness to drive inside make him an important offensive catalyst.

name: Lars Eller
summary: Lars Eller remains a steady veteran who provides leadership and defensive reliability. While his offensive numbers are modest, his experience and discipline make him a trusted center in key moments.

name: Jack Roslovic
summary: Jack Roslovic’s speed and puck control fit well within Carolina’s up-tempo style. He helps drive possession and transition play, giving the Hurricanes extra pace down the middle.

name: Curtis Lazar
summary: Curtis Lazar brings energy and accountability to every shift. While his offensive ceiling is limited, his defensive responsibility and physical forechecking make him an effective role player.

name: Aliaksei Protas
summary: Aliaksei Protas has shown flashes of offensive potential, using his size and reach to create scoring chances. As his confidence grows, he’s becoming a more noticeable threat in Washington’s lineup.

name: Connor Murphy
summary: Connor Murphy continues to be a steady defensive presence on a young Chicago roster. His shot-blocking and physical play set the tone in his own end night after night.

name: Dylan Cozens
summary: Dylan Cozens brings power and determination to his game. He plays physically, drives the net hard, and has developed into a reliable two-way contributor.

name: Ivan Provorov
summary: Ivan Provorov has a mixed profile, logging heavy minutes but struggling with turnovers. His willingness to take risks with the puck sometimes backfires, yet he remains a key figure on Columbus’s blue line.

name: Paul Cotter
summary: Paul Cotter is a physical forward who thrives on energy and contact. His aggressive forecheck and high motor bring intensity to every shift.

name: Ty Emberson
summary: Ty Emberson is a steady defender with a physical edge. His combination of sound positioning and willingness to play the body gives Edmonton valuable depth on defense.

name: Kevin Stenlund
summary: Kevin Stenlund plays a responsible two-way game, contributing through defensive awareness and shot blocking. He quietly fills important depth minutes down the middle.

name: Rasmus Andersson
summary: Rasmus Andersson is the heartbeat of Calgary’s defense, combining high-end shot blocking with steady offensive contribution. He logs big minutes and rarely shies away from difficult matchups.

name: Sean Walker
summary: Sean Walker’s smooth skating and strong puck control allow Carolina to dominate possession when he’s on the ice. His calm, efficient play makes him a valuable modern defenseman.

name: Mathew Barzal
summary: Mathew Barzal remains one of the most dynamic skaters in the NHL. His creativity and vision consistently drive the Islanders’ offense and open space for his linemates.

name: Viktor Arvidsson
summary: Viktor Arvidsson provides a spark with his relentless speed and high work rate. Though injuries have slowed him at times, his energy and offensive instincts remain impactful.

name: Jesper Bratt
summary: Jesper Bratt’s elite playmaking and speed make him one of New Jersey’s offensive cornerstones. His ability to create scoring chances for himself and others keeps defenses constantly guessing.

name: Tomas Hertl
summary: Tomas Hertl dominates around the net with power and poise. His ability to finish in traffic and protect the puck down low makes him a consistent scoring threat.

name: Jesper Boqvist
summary: Jesper Boqvist has added physicality to his skilled game, using his speed to pressure opponents and create turnovers. His versatility makes him a strong fit for Florida’s deep lineup.

name: Mason Lohrei
summary: Mason Lohrei shows confidence with the puck and a strong feel for the game. His poise and vision give Boston another young defenseman capable of handling big moments.

name: Brandon Duhaime
summary: Brandon Duhaime brings energy and edge to every shift. His aggressive style sometimes leads to penalties, but he’s invaluable for his forechecking and willingness to play tough minutes.

name: Mats Zuccarello
summary: Mats Zuccarello continues to be an elite playmaker with unmatched creativity. His chemistry with teammates and knack for creating scoring chances make him essential to Minnesota’s offense.

name: Neal Pionk
summary: Neal Pionk provides steady production from the blue line. His willingness to jump into the play and contribute offensively adds balance to Winnipeg’s defense.

name: Zachary L'Heureux
summary: Zachary L’Heureux plays with an edge and thrives in physical battles. His hard-nosed approach energizes Nashville and wears down opponents.

name: Max Domi
summary: Max Domi brings his trademark fire and competitiveness to every shift. He walks the line between agitation and offense, often sparking his team with emotion and creativity.

name: Kurtis MacDermid
summary: Kurtis MacDermid serves as an enforcer and physical presence for New Jersey. His size and toughness provide protection and energy for a young roster.

name: Eetu Luostarinen
summary: Eetu Luostarinen brings strong two-way play and physical edge to Florida’s lineup. He’s dependable in all zones and helps drive possession through smart, disciplined hockey.

name: Mattias Ekholm
summary: Ekholm’s calm, efficient play continues to shine for Edmonton. With strong puck-possession numbers and one of the better takeaway rates among defensemen, he’s driving play in the right direction and breaking up chances with veteran poise.

name: Robert Thomas
summary: Thomas has become the heartbeat of St. Louis’ attack, piling up assists and points at an elite rate. He’s heavily involved in puck movement and playmaking, though his high giveaway total reflects his aggressive style as a primary creator.

name: Moritz Seider
summary: Seider anchors Detroit’s blue line with physical edge and elite two-way impact. He blocks shots, delivers hits, and contributes offensively, drawing penalties and generating consistent pressure. His all-around game places him among the top young defensemen in hockey.

name: Tyler Seguin
summary: Seguin continues to be a steady presence in Dallas’ top six, helping drive scoring chances when he’s on the ice. His strong expected goals share highlights his ability to tilt the ice in the Stars’ favor.

name: Mark Kastelic
summary: Kastelic brings grit and energy to Boston’s lineup. He hits hard, draws penalties, and isn’t afraid to mix it up physically, giving the Bruins valuable depth toughness.

name: Jack Hughes
summary: Hughes continues to dazzle as the Devils’ offensive engine. His combination of shot volume, speed, and creativity keeps defenders guessing, and his control of play metrics underline just how much he drives New Jersey’s attack.

name: Jared Spurgeon
summary: Spurgeon remains a reliable force on Minnesota’s back end. When he’s on the ice, the Wild control play at a high rate thanks to his skating, puck movement, and smart defensive positioning.

name: Jacob Moverare
summary: Moverare quietly gives the Kings solid minutes on defense. His numbers show that Los Angeles tends to control play with him out there, a reflection of his steady, mistake-free approach.

name: Jonathan Huberdeau
summary: Huberdeau’s playmaking instincts remain sharp, leading to strong assist numbers and drawn penalties. He’s getting involved defensively too, blocking shots and supporting Calgary’s transition game.

name: Alex Killorn
summary: Killorn’s veteran presence remains valuable for Anaheim. He’s contributing to the Ducks’ offense both through expected and actual goals, bringing his usual mix of consistency and leadership.

name: Mason McTavish
summary: McTavish continues to grow into a key role for Anaheim. He’s creating offense through secondary assists and driving play toward the net, using his strength to earn scoring opportunities and draw penalties.

name: Matvei Michkov
summary: Michkov’s offensive flair has quickly translated to the NHL. He’s generating high-danger looks and rebounds, making defenders pay with his quick release and fearless shooting approach.

name: Logan Stankoven
summary: Stankoven’s early numbers suggest a strong two-way forward. He’s active around the net, drawing penalties and winning possession battles, showing poise beyond his age.

name: Gustav Nyquist
summary: Nyquist provides veteran stability and scoring touch on Minnesota’s wings. His on-ice goal share reflects his value as a dependable contributor in both directions.

name: Ryan Lomberg
summary: Lomberg has logged heavy minutes and continues to bring relentless energy. He’s the kind of depth player who sets the tone through effort and physical play.

name: Adam Gaudette
summary: Gaudette’s high time on bench reflects limited usage, but he’s been a flexible depth option capable of filling gaps when called upon.

name: Ryan Donato
summary: Donato’s nose for the net has been on display in Chicago. With solid goal totals and timely assists, he’s carving out a consistent offensive role for the rebuilding Blackhawks.

name: Alexander Kerfoot
summary: Kerfoot continues to show his reliability as a two-way forward. He blocks shots, wins takeaways, and contributes in all situations — the kind of steady play that coaches trust.

name: Thomas Harley
summary: Harley’s breakout season is driven by excellent skating and offensive instincts. He’s generating offense from the blue line while maintaining strong on-ice results, giving Dallas a dynamic option on defense.

name: Connor McMichael
summary: McMichael has emerged as a dangerous offensive threat for Washington. His ability to create rebounds and convert them into goals makes him a constant presence around the crease.

name: Elias Lindholm
summary: Lindholm’s all-around game fits seamlessly in Boston. He blocks shots, creates chances, and helps drive scoring plays, balancing offense and defense with veteran precision.

name: Beck Malenstyn
summary: Malenstyn brings grit and effort to Buffalo’s bottom six. His heavy hitting and willingness to block shots make him a valuable energy player who does the hard work quietly.

name: Dylan Guenther
summary: Guenther is emerging as a key offensive weapon for Utah. His high shot volume and quality possession metrics show a player ready to become a top-line scoring threat.

name: Shane Wright
summary: Wright’s defensive awareness continues to stand out. Blocking shots and supporting his zone well, he’s developing into the kind of reliable two-way center Seattle can build around.

name: Colton Parayko
summary: Parayko continues to contribute offensively from the back end, chipping in goals and high-danger chances. His combination of size, skating, and confidence makes him a steady top-four option.

name: Alexander Holtz
summary: Holtz has shown encouraging signs in limited action with Vegas. When he’s on the ice, the team tends to control play, suggesting strong underlying potential as he earns more ice time.

name: Kyle Palmieri
summary: Palmieri continues to be a dependable offensive piece for the Islanders. He’s creating rebounds and converting chances, using his veteran instincts to stay effective in key moments.

name: Alex DeBrincat
summary: DeBrincat remains one of Detroit’s most dynamic forwards, combining volume shooting with high conversion. He generates dangerous looks every night and drives play with relentless pace.

name: Roope Hintz
summary: Hintz’s quickness and vision make him one of Dallas’ most effective play drivers. He consistently finds teammates for prime chances and adds a steady scoring touch himself.

name: Pavel Dorofeyev
summary: Dorofeyev has broken out as an efficient scorer for Vegas, blending creativity with a strong sense for space. His high expected goals numbers back up his steady production.

name: Jordan Martinook
summary: Martinook’s value extends beyond the scoresheet. Carolina dominates play when he’s off the ice, but his energy and forechecking still help set the tone for his line.

name: Jared McCann
summary: McCann provides reliable offense for Seattle, generating a high volume of shots and creating second-chance opportunities. His scoring touch remains a constant threat.

name: Kiefer Sherwood
summary: Sherwood has been a physical force for Vancouver, leading the league in hits. He plays a hard, north-south game that wears down opponents and sparks his team’s energy.

name: Logan Stanley
summary: Logan Stanley’s metrics suggest the Jets generate quality scoring chances when he’s off the ice, but his defensive presence still provides stability. His numbers indicate that he plays a conservative role, helping keep games tight.

name: Matt Boldy
summary: Matt Boldy is a high-volume shooter who creates constant pressure on opposing goalies. He fires from every area of the ice, with over 550 shot attempts and a strong expected goal output. His ability to force turnovers and generate rebounds shows an active two-way style, though his high giveaway numbers suggest occasional risk-taking in possession.

name: Pavel Zacha
summary: Pavel Zacha thrives in high-scoring situations, with the Bruins generating nearly a hundred goals while he’s on the ice. His line consistently produces quality opportunities, showing his value as a steady contributor in Boston’s offense.

name: William Karlsson
summary: William Karlsson’s numbers indicate that Vegas maintains strong scoring potential even when he’s not on the ice, reflecting how he supports deep forward rotations. His role balances smart puck play with dependable defensive structure.

name: Adam Fox
summary: Adam Fox is among the league’s elite puck-moving defensemen, driving offense through playmaking and controlled possession. His assist totals and strong Fenwick and Corsi rates show how he pushes the pace and keeps the puck in New York’s favor. Offensively, he’s dangerous at generating high-quality shots and rebound chances, turning defense into quick scoring plays.

name: Noah Dobson
summary: Noah Dobson continues to fire from the point with consistency, registering nearly 200 shots on goal and over 400 attempts. His strong puck control and above-average Fenwick and Corsi scores suggest he drives play in the Islanders’ favor. Despite some giveaways, he balances his offense with timely takeaways and effective puck recovery.

name: Sean Kuraly
summary: Sean Kuraly logs heavy bench time, reflecting a depth role in Columbus’s lineup. His presence provides reliable support in a limited but steady capacity for the team’s rotation.

name: Alexis Lafrenire
summary: Alexis Lafrenière’s on-ice goal totals point to consistent offensive involvement. The Rangers generate strong scoring numbers with him on the ice, highlighting his continued growth as a key contributor.

name: Johnathan Kovacevic
summary: Johnathan Kovacevic plays a physical game, evident from his high penalty total. His aggressive defensive approach can disrupt opponents but occasionally leads to time spent in the box.

name: Bowen Byram
summary: Bowen Byram has been an active force offensively from the blue line, contributing to over a hundred goals while on the ice. His ability to generate high-danger shots and support scoring plays through secondary assists highlights a balanced, attack-minded game.

name: Phillip Danault
summary: Phillip Danault brings his trademark two-way consistency, blocking a high number of shots while also setting up teammates effectively. His primary assists and defensive commitment underline his value as a playmaker who contributes on both sides of the puck.

name: Carter Verhaeghe
summary: Carter Verhaeghe dominates possession and scoring chances for Florida, boasting top-tier Corsi and Fenwick percentages. He fires pucks from all over the ice, generating both volume and quality. His game blends aggression and control, though his penalty and giveaway counts show a player constantly pushing the pace.

name: JJ Peterka
summary: JJ Peterka’s offensive instincts shine through his high assist numbers and consistent involvement in Buffalo’s goals. While he occasionally turns the puck over, his ability to set up plays and contribute on both ends gives him strong two-way value.

name: Alex Laferriere
summary: Alex Laferriere finds success near the net, generating second-chance opportunities and rebound goals. His possession metrics show he helps control play for Los Angeles, while his missed shots suggest he isn’t afraid to fire often from a range of areas.

name: Joel Armia
summary: Joel Armia sees moderate usage but continues to serve as a steady depth option. His limited ice time reflects a supporting role where consistency and defensive awareness are key.

name: Cole Koepke
summary: Cole Koepke brings physicality to Boston’s lineup, recording a high number of hits. His game centers on energy and forechecking, giving his team a boost in puck battles and board play.

name: Mika Zibanejad
summary: Mika Zibanejad is one of the Rangers’ most productive forwards, driving offense through assists and heavy shot volume. His presence on the ice correlates with major scoring output, while his mix of rebounds and takeaways highlights his two-way engagement.

name: Adam Larsson
summary: Adam Larsson’s defensive steadiness allows Seattle to thrive when he’s on the ice. The team’s strong goal and chance metrics show how his presence supports play in both ends of the rink.

name: Darnell Nurse
summary: Darnell Nurse plays a complete, high-activity game for Edmonton. His strong shot totals and physical play are backed by solid possession numbers, showing he supports both puck movement and zone control. While aggressive, his penalties come as a product of his assertive defensive style.

name: Emil Lilleberg
summary: Emil Lilleberg’s season has been defined by physical intensity. His high number of penalties shows an aggressive edge, but his ability to draw calls himself proves he’s a disruptive force who plays on the line.

name: Kirill Marchenko
summary: Kirill Marchenko blends scoring touch with responsible play, racking up over 30 goals while contributing heavily to blocked shots and takeaways. His shot volume ranks near the top of the league, showing how frequently he creates pressure in the offensive zone.

name: Quinn Hughes
summary: Quinn Hughes continues to prove himself as one of the league’s best defensemen. His elite assist and point totals show top-end offensive vision, while outstanding Corsi and Fenwick metrics highlight how much Vancouver controls play when he’s on the ice. Hughes drives puck possession and chance creation at a rare level for his position.

name: Connor Clifton
summary: Connor Clifton brings grit to Buffalo’s defense with over 200 recorded hits. His physical play sets the tone and keeps opposing forwards under pressure in the defensive zone.

name: Eric Robinson
summary: Eric Robinson’s underlying metrics suggest that Carolina performs well when he’s off the ice, pointing to strong team depth around him. His limited usage still reflects value in defensive and energy-driven shifts.

name: William Eklund
summary: William Eklund is emerging as a creative playmaker for San Jose. His high assist rates and on-ice goal production show his ability to facilitate offense and read developing plays effectively.

name: Kasperi Kapanen
summary: Kasperi Kapanen’s numbers suggest Edmonton generates strong expected scoring chances during his time off the ice, but his skating and transition play help maintain balance when he’s in the lineup.
